full_response:
Let  

• N = the event that a single roll is “neutral’’ (sum ∈ {2,3,4,6,7});  
• A = the event that a single roll is 5 (Asami’s winning roll);  
• B = the event that a single roll is “big’’ (sum ≥ 8, i.e. 8,9,10,11,12).  

Counting the 36 equally–likely outcomes of two fair dice,

	#(A) = 4  → Pr[A] = 4/36 = 1/9  
	#(B) = 15 → Pr[B] = 15/36 = 5/12  
	#(N) = 17 → Pr[N] = 17/36.  

These three events form a partition of the sample space for one roll.

Definition of Eₙ  
Eₙ occurs iff  

	• rolls 1,…,n–1 are all neutral (no 5, no ≥8), and  
	• roll n is a 5.

Because consecutive rolls are independent,

	Pr[Eₙ]
	= Pr[N]^{\,n-1} · Pr[A]
	= (17/36)^{\,n-1} · (1/9).

Hence, for every integer n ≥ 1,

\boxed{\displaystyle \Pr[E_n] \;=\; \frac{1}{9}\left(\frac{17}{36}\right)^{n-1}} 

Confidence: 0.35
